name: Bud Scott
description: American jazz guitarist, banjoist and singer (1890–1949)
abstract: Arthur Budd Scott (January 11, 1890 – July 2, 1949) was an American jazz guitarist, banjoist and singer. He was one of the earliest musicians associated with the New Orleans jazz scene.

concerts.label: Kid Ory and His Creole Jazz Band

works.t: Bill Bailey, Won't You Please Come Home?
I'm Busy and You Can't Come In

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Shoffner
about_quotes.evidence: And then I spent a band we struck that was Joe Oliver, myself, Paul Baldwin, Louis Russell, Bud Scott, our, our, our, Donnell Howard, Albert Nicholas, and our, Bonnie.

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: The personnel of that band was Harold Brown, pianist, Bud Scott on banjo and guitar... The leader and director was a saxophonist and violinist, Leon Herniford.

about_quotes.speaker: George Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Bud. Because I worked with Bud quite a bit around here... And I worked with him with a couple of bands around there.

about_quotes.speaker: Minor "Ram" Hall
about_quotes.evidence: We had Bud Scott, Papa Mutt, Eddie Garland, myself, Ory, and I think we had Fred Washington for a while.

about_quotes.speaker: George "Pops" Foster
about_quotes.evidence: He had Billy Butler, a guy called Bonner, myself, Mutt, Bud Scott, and he had a very good band.

about_quotes.speaker: Barney Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: It was Albert Nicholas and Paul Barberin. And who else was in it? Bud Scott. That's right.
